update.description: The Way of Black Smoke Sub-class from Continentia.
update.author: Corie Welch and the Amazing Dicey Dudes

description.p: Those who follow this tradition are masters with their guns and capable of turning their misfortunate misfires against their opponents.
Many monks of this tradition take great pride in their firearm and take extra care to know it inside and out.

sheet.description: You follow a monastic tradition that emphasizes mastery with a firearm.

description.p: Beginning at 3rd level when you choose this tradition, you gain proficiency in Simple and Martial Light Firearms, as well as Gunsmith’s Tools. Light firearms are considered Monk Weapons.

sheet.description: Light firearms are considered Monk Weapons.

description.p: Beginning at 3rd level when you choose this tradition you may choose two abilities from the following list. You gain two more abilities at 6th level, 11th level, and 17th level.
When using the Flurry of Blows ability, you may instead make two attacks with a light firearm or one unarmed strike and one attack with a light firearm.

sheet.description: When using the Flurry of Blows ability, you may instead make two attacks with a light firearm or one unarmed strike and one attack with a light firearm.

description.p: When making an attack with a light firearm, you may spend 2 ki points to allow the bullet to move in any direction equal to its range. The range of the attack is halved. Unless impossible, the attack ignores all cover.

sheet.description: When making an attack with a light firearm, you may spend 2 ki points to allow the bullet to move in any direction equal to its range. The range of the attack is halved. Unless impossible, the attack ignores all cover.

description.p: After successfully making a check with deflect missiles, you may make an additional attack with a light firearm as part of the reaction.

sheet.description: After successfully making a check with deflect missiles, you may make an additional attack with a light firearm as part of the reaction.

description.p: You may expend 1 ki point to reload a light firearm you currently have equipped without using an action.

sheet.description: You may expend 1 ki point to reload a light firearm you currently have equipped without using an action.

description.p: When rolling for damage with a light firearm, you may expend 3 ki points to do maximum damage instead of rolling.

sheet.description: When rolling for damage with a light firearm, you may expend 3 ki points to do maximum damage instead of rolling.

description.p: You may add your Wisdom modifier to your initiative rolls.

sheet.description: You may add your Wisdom modifier to your initiative rolls.

description.p: When you use your Patient Defense ability, you may make an attack with a light firearm as a reaction if an enemy misses an attack against you or if you succeed at a Dexterity saving throw.

sheet.description: When you use your Patient Defense ability, you may make an attack with a light firearm as a reaction if an enemy misses an attack against you or if you succeed at a Dexterity saving throw.

description.p: When you use your Step of the Wind ability, you gain +2 to your AC until the beginning of your next turn.

sheet.description: When you use your Step of the Wind ability, you gain +2 to your AC until the beginning of your next turn.

description.p: Beginning at 3rd level, when you choose this tradition, you learn how to turn a mistake into a tactical advantage. When you roll a critical failure (1) while making an attack with a firearm, you instead roll a percentile die on the Master of Misfortune table instead of the Misfire Table.

tr.td: Your firearm explodes dealing damage equal to twice the damage dice of the firearm to yourself in fire damage. The firearm becomes unusable until you make a successful DC 15 Gunsmith's Tools check during a long rest.

You can feel that your firearm is about to explode; as a reaction, you may spend 1 ki to throw the firearm at a range of 20/60 feet. You are considered proficient in this attack. The firearm will explode dealing twice the amount of damage dice of the firearm to the target in fire damage. The firearm becomes unusable until you make a successful DC 15 Gunsmith's Tools check during a long rest.
Your firearm jams and becomes unusable until you make a successful DC 10 Tinker's or Gunsmith's Tools check as an action.
Your firearm jams and becomes unusable until you make a successful DC 8 Tinker's or Gunsmith's Tools check as a bonus action.
Your firearm does not fire, but will work fine on your next attack.

The bullet shatters mid air when fired. All creatures in a 20x15 cone in front of you must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, they take 1d4 piercing damage, and half as much on a successful one.
The gun fires rapidly seemingly out of nowhere. You may make a number of attacks at disadvantage equal to the amount of bullets you have in the gun. Your attack action ends after this.
You slip. Reroll the attack at disadvantage against a different target.
Reroll the attack. If you hit, you deal an additional 1d4 fire damage.
All of your bullets fall out of your gun.
Reroll the attack. You may add your Wisdom modifier to the roll.
Reroll the attack. If it hits, the attack ignores resistances.
You deal critical damage to the chosen target.

sheet.description: When you roll a critical failure (1) while making an attack with a firearm, you instead roll a percentile die on the Master of Misfortune table instead of the Misfire Table.
